page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6.13xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 52 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CL processor using the x86_64 architecture. With a one-to-one mapping of threads to physical cores, the instance provides 52 vCPUs without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.77 GB per vCPU.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial RAM capacity relative to compute resources, allowing organizations to run large in-memory datasets without paying for excess CPU cores.

The ecs.re6.13xlarge is engineered for workloads requiring massive memory footprints. Appropriate use cases include in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data-processing pipelines that fit entirely within system memory.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6.13xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 52 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 52 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CL processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 14.77 GB per core. It does not include local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The hardware configuration is optimized for memory-dense applications, offering a resource-efficient profile for workloads that demand substantial memory capacity without proportional compute scaling. This instance is highly suited for running in-memory databases, large-scale caching systems, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data analytics.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and massive RAM capacity for demanding in-memory enterprise workloads.
